"https://api.github.com/users/vnykmshr/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/vnykmshr", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44973", "cvss": {"score": 8.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}], "description": "### Impact
Multiple path traversal issues exist across different components of `go-billy`. Insufficient path sanitization and boundary enforcement may allow crafted paths (e.g., using `..`) to escape intended base directories.

While go-billy was not originally designed to provide a strong security boundary, some of these issues were inconsistent across some of the built-in implementations. This results in scenarios where applications relying on `go-billy` for some level of isolation may inadvertently expose access to unintended filesystem locations.

The `osfs.ChrootOS` implementation is notably affected by this vulnerability and is now deprecated in `v5`, removed at `v6`. Users are recommended to move on to `osfs.BoundOS` instead: `osfs.New(path, WithBoundOS())`.

Users requiring stronger security boundary enforcement are recommended to upgrade to `v6`, where the `osfs` implementation are backed by the [traversal-resistant](https://go.dev/blog/osroot) primitive [os.Root](https://pkg.go.dev/os#Root).

### Patches
Users should upgrade to a patched version in order to mitigate this vulnerability. Versions prior to `v5` are likely to be affected, users are recommended to upgrade to a supported `go-billy` version.

### Credits
Thanks to @faran66 and @vnykmshr for finding and separately reporting this issue privately to the go-git project. 🙇", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0031, "percentile": 0.22579}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-qw64-3x98-g7q2", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-14T18:25:38Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qw64-3x98-g7q2", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-qw64-3x98-g7q2"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44973"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-28T22:16:59Z", "published_at": "2026-05-14T18:25:38Z", "references": ["https://github.com/go-git/go-billy/security/advisories/GHSA-qw64-3x98-g7q2", "https://github.com/go-git/go-billy/releases/tag/v5.9.0", "https://github.com/go-git/go-billy/releases/tag/v6.0.0-alpha.1", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44973", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qw64-3x98-g7q2"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/go-git/go-billy/security-advisories/GHSA-qw64-3x98-g7q2", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/go-git/go-billy", "summary": "go-billy has path traversal vulnerabilities", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-09T10:26:28Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-qw64-3x98-g7q2", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "5.9.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/go-git/go-billy/v5"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 5.9.0"}, {"first_patched_version": "6.0.0-alpha.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "go", "name": "github.com/go-git/go-billy/v6"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 6.0.0-alpha.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: go-billy has path traversal vulnerabilities
